rationale: The citing paper uses Dhar and Wertenbroch (2000) in its theoretical setup to justify treating bundle components as hedonic versus utilitarian, which is central to its hypotheses and experimental design. It also cites that work as evidence that hedonic purchases are harder to justify and can be shifted by justification-related factors, supporting the proposed guilt-based mechanism. This is not just a background list citation; it meaningfully informs how the 2010 paper frames and explains its core effect.
relation_description: The 2010 study builds on Dhar and Wertenbroch (2000) as a conceptual foundation for hedonic versus utilitarian choice and justification dynamics. It uses that foundation to develop and test a new pricing-frame effect in cross-category bundles.
